page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is built on the Nitro hypervisor and provides 64.0 GB of system memory, translating to a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for multi-core operations, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48563.42 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11689.0. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 973.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1519.38. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate strong read speeds of 1894.04 GB/sec for small cached blocks, but large uncached write speeds drop to 74.98 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the c6gn.8xlarge stems from its dedicated ARM physical cores, which eliminate hyperthreading resource contention and offer high compute density per resource unit. Tradeoffs include weak single-threaded performance in compression tasks like gzip and weak prompt processing speeds in LLM inference. Lacking local NVMe storage, it relies on network storage performance. This instance is best suited for network-intensive compute workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and distributed database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of system memory and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48563.42 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11689.0, though single-core performance remains weak. The instance offers a cost-efficient hardware profile for multi-threaded workloads due to its dedicated core allocation and high network throughput. It is suited for network-heavy compute tasks, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced multi-core performance for network-heavy workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and high-speed fifty gigabit baseline network bandwidth for distributed workloads.
